prevalence.trend: Seasonal peaks in winter; stable overall with slight increase due to post-COVID effects

incidence.source: ECDC Surveillance Reports and WHO European Regional Office data

burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2021 (IHME) adapted for EU

vulnerablePopulations: Individuals with chronic respiratory conditions (e.g., asthma)
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis based on symptoms such as sore throat, nasal congestion, cough, and fever; confirmed by rapid antigen tests for streptococcal infections or viral PCR if needed

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: N/A (no routine screening; symptomatic case management)
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: High for viral cases (symptomatic relief >90%); moderate for bacterial due to resistance (70-80%)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Follows EU harmonized guidelines (e.g., NICE/ECDC protocols) emphasizing clinical assessment over routine testing to reduce overuse
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Symptomatic treatment preferred; antibiotics only for confirmed bacterial etiology (e.g., Group A Streptococcus); adherence to AWaRe classification by WHO
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Rising antibiotic resistance (e.g., >20% resistance to macrolides in Streptococcus pneumoniae per ECDC data); seasonal overload on healthcare systems; disparities in access across Eastern vs. Western EU
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Robust surveillance via ECDC networks; high vaccination coverage for preventable causes (e.g., influenza >50%); strong public health campaigns on AMR
